summary: Israel's posture is set by the Israel National Cyber Directorate, whose best-practices guidance directs organisations on achieving cyber readiness for the post-quantum age and migrating to quantum-resistant cryptography. The picture has since broadened across government: the National Digital Agency has issued guidance directing government ministries and public bodies to prepare for the post-quantum era, map their infrastructure, assess data exposure and move towards quantum-resistant encryption, and the Ministry of Communications has published an intelligence report analysing how quantum computers threaten common encryption. In the financial sector the Bank of Israel has gone furthest, with a Banking Supervision letter directing banking corporations to map their cryptographic inventory and submit an initial preparedness plan. No sovereign Israeli post-quantum scheme or specific algorithm is mandated nationally.
migrationTimeline: 2026 | Banking corporations submit an initial post-quantum preparedness plan, within a year of the January 2025 Banking Supervision letter
govActors:
INCD | Israel National Cyber Directorate, best-practices guidance on organizational readiness for the post-quantum age
Bank of Israel | Banking Supervision Department, supervisory letter directing banking corporations to prepare for the quantum computing era
National Digital Agency (Israel) | policy guidance directing government ministries and public bodies to prepare for the post-quantum era
Ministry of Communications (Israel), Sectoral Cyber Unit | intelligence report on quantum computers and their impact on information security
mainRegulation:
Bank of Israel Banking Supervision letter on quantum-era preparedness (7 January 2025) | national | binding-by-market-access
INCD Organizational Cyber Readiness to the Post-Quantum Age (best practices) | national | guidance
National Digital Agency guidance: Preparedness for Dealing with Encryption in the Quantum Computing Era | national | guidance
obligation: Supervised banking corporations must assess the suitability of their infrastructure for post-quantum encryption, map their cryptographic inventory and submit an initial preparedness plan with board-level approval to the Banking Supervision Department, within a year of the January 2025 letter. This obligation is confined to the banking sector. Israel National Cyber Directorate material applies economy-wide but is advisory, and no specific post-quantum algorithm is mandated nationally.
classificationBasis:
summary + govActors (INCD): INCD Best Practices - Organizational Cyber Readiness to the Post-Quantum Age (included results row)
summary + govActors (Bank of Israel) + migrationTimeline: Bank of Israel Banking Supervision letter of 7 January 2025 directing banks to assess infrastructure, map cryptographic inventory and submit an initial preparedness plan within a year (included results row); the 2026 entry is that within-a-year deadline, a sectoral directive rather than a national completion date
